title: eryngium sparganophyllum
intro: Eryngium sparganophyllum, also known as Sea Holly, is a perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its blue-green foliage and distinctive thistle-like flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and inflammation, due to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Eryngium sparganophyllum include reducing symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, alleviating menstrual cramps, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
